judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the final execute_python step (call_SfK1uhwib60lUykVqbLSrbU5), the agent parsed GitHub repo names and star counts from the free‑text Project_Information using brittle regexes and then aggregated by taking the max Stars across multiple mappings, misattributing stars (e.g., to styled-components/quill) and producing a top 5 that differs from the ground truth.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent implemented a correct overall plan but, in the final execute_python step, extracted GitHub star counts from project_info using an overly strict regex (e.g., r'(?:has|with) (?:a total of )?([0-9,]+) stars') that fails on texts like "has 521 open issues, 38715 stars," resulting in many NaNs being dropped and an incorrect top-5 selection.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the final compute step (execute_python call_QEYMlqIKYZscSyG1sGIe6Wzi), the agent used a brittle regex to extract ProjectName and star counts from project_info.Project_Information that fails on variants like “The project is hosted on GitHub under the name learnfrontend-dc/product-cart…”, causing many missing stars and yielding the wrong top-5 packages.

B. Failure modes encountered:

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent merged latest NPM versions with all entries in project_packageversion without filtering by ProjectType/RelationType (see query_db call_3BqG0mb5aUcX1QbK0fDxNUVo and execute_python call_W5sJUQTaOsF2kJTssxMgMOlq), causing association to unrelated high-star projects and thus an incorrect top-5 list compared to the ground truth.

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent’s plan was sound, but in execute_python it extracted GitHub stars from project_info.Project_Information using a brittle regex ("The project (.*?) ... (?:has|currently has).*? ([0-9,]+) stars") that misses many formats (e.g., “with 8073 stars” or “a stars count of 57779”), causing incorrect/missing star values and thus the wrong top-5 list.
